title: Multi-pathogen detection & quantification using digital PCR
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"993f1","text":"This protocol is optimised for quantifying RNA and DNA targets using the QIAcuity OneStep Advanced Probe Kit with hydrolysis probes in singleplex or multiplex (up to two targets) reactions on QIAGEN’s QIAcuity digital PCR (dPCR) instruments. It also includes instructions for preparing in-house 20× primer–probe assays. This version includes our in-house designed hepatitis B detection and quantification assay. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"bcf21","text":"Refer to the QIAcuity User Manual and QIAcuity User Manual Extension for guidance on assay design and experimental setup for the QIAcuity platform.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"51ibu","text":"The QIAcuity OneStep Advanced Probe Kit has been specially formulated with a hot-start RT enzyme, allowing users to assemble reactions at room temperature","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4653s","text":"The Enhancer GC is recommended for use with all Applied Biosystems TaqMan assays, amplicons \u003e150 bp in length, GC-rich amplicons, and RNA targets containing challenging secondary structures.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"vu1v","text":"1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6lprg","text":"Standard 96-well plate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7go7i","text":"Microseal Adhesive Seal","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2psn2","text":"QIAcuity 8.5K, 96-well Nanoplate ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fimjn","text":"Pipettes of different sizes ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"atd8s","text":"Multichannel pipettes ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7uan5","text":"QIAcuity OneStep Advanced Probe Kit ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Place the following on ice to thaw: 100x OneStep Advanced Reverse Transcription Mix, 4x QIAcuity OneStep Advanced Probe Master Mix Template RNA Primer-probe Mix Enhancer GC RNase-Free Water
Using the calculations in Table 1, prepare a master mix in a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube. Remember to add 10% of each component to account for pipetting errors.

Table 1: Assay composition Component x1 sample x? samples Final concentration 1 4x OneStep Advanced Probe Master Mix 3 µL µL 1x 2 100x OneStep Advanced RT Mix 0.12 µL µL 1x 3 20x primer-probe mix 1 0.6 µL µL 0.4 µM forward primer 0.4 µM reverse primer 0.2 µM probe 4 20x primer-probe mix 2* (for multiplex) 0.6 µL µL 0.4 µM forward primer 0.4 µM reverse primer 0.2 probe 5 Enhancer GC 1 µL µL - 6 Total reaction volume 5.32 µL µL - ? = number of samples + controls + 10%, * = substitute with RNase-Free water for singleplex assay

Vortex the reaction mix thoroughly and briefly centrifuge. Dispense 5.32 μL of the reaction mix into each well of a standard 96-well PCR pre-plate (according to your experiment layout). The pre-plate may be assembled at room temperature.
Add 8 µL template RNA to wells containing the reaction mix. Thoroughly mix the template RNA with the reaction mix by pipetting up and down.
Seal the pre-plate with a microseal adhesive suitable for standard 96-well plates and briefly centrifuge (1000 rpm for 30 seconds) to collect any droplets.

Transfer 13.32 μL from each well of the pre-plate to a 96-well QIAcuity Nanoplate, taking care to avoid introducing bubbles. Always place the Nanoplate on a Nanoplate Tray when handling to protect the sensitive imaging surface at the bottom. Do not place the Nanoplate on ice at any time—work at room temperature.

Seal the Nanoplate with the seal provided in the kit, load it into the QIAcuity instrument, and initiate the one-step RT-dPCR cycling program below. This cycling program works for all* different primer-probe combinations, despite their different melting temperatures. This is possible because of

Table 2: QIAcuity RT-dPCR cycling program Step Time Temperature 1 Reverse Transcription 40 min 50°C 2 RT Enzyme Inactivation 2 min 95°C 3 2-step cycling (45 cycles) - - 4 Denaturation 5 sec 95°C 5 Combined annealing & Extension 1 min 60°C
Vigorously mix the individual solutions and centrifuge the tubes briefly to settle the liquids
If you choose not to purchase QIAcuity pathogen detection assays from QIAGEN, you may source your own primers and probes and prepare them as 100 μM stock solutions.
Thaw your primers and probe on ice and prepare a 20x assay as indicated in Table 3 below.

Table 3: Preparing 20x primer-probe assays Component Final Conc. in 20x assay Volume to add 1 Forward primer (100 μM) 8 μM 8 μL 2 Reverse primer (100 μM) 8 μM 8 μL 3 Probe (100 μM) 4 μM 4 μL 4 Nuclease-free water - 80 μL 5 Total - 100 μL

Influenza A assay Primer/probe Sequence (5'-3') 1 InfA For1 CAAGACCAATCYTGTCACCTCTGAC 2 InfA Rev1 GCATTYTGGACAAAVCGTCTACG 3 InfA Probe /56-FAM/TGCAGTCCT/ZEN/CGCTCACTGGGCACG/3IABkFQ/ Research Use Only CDC Influenza SARS-CoV-2 (Flu SC2) Multiplex Assay Primers and Probes

Pan-measles assay Primer/probe Sequence (5'-3') 1 MVN1139F TGGCATCTGAACTCGGTATCAC 2 MVN1213R TGTCCTCAGTAGTATGCATTGCAA 3 MVNP1163Probe-2 /56-TAMN/CCGAGGATGCAAGGCTTGTTTCAGA/3BHQ_1/ Hummel, K.B., Lowe, L., Bellini, W.J. and Rota, P.A., 2006. Development of quantitative gene-specific real-time RT-PCR assays for the detection of measles virus in clinical specimens. Journal of virological methods , 132 (1-2), pp.166-173.

Influenza B assay Primer/probe Sequence (5'-3') 1 InfB For TCCTCAAYTCACTCTTCGAGCG 2 InfB Rev CGGTGCTCTTGACCAAATTGG 3 InfB Probe /5YakYel/CCAATTCGA/ZEN/GCAGCTGAAACTGCGGTG/3IABkFQ/ Research Use Only CDC Influenza SARS-CoV-2 (Flu SC2) Multiplex Assay Primers and Probes

All primers and probes are sourced from IDT through Whitehead Scientific. The reporters, internal quenchers, and 3′-end quenchers used in the assays below may be proprietary to IDT. When designing assays, carefully consider the compatibility of the selected reporters and quenchers, and exercise particular caution when planning multiplex assays to avoid cross-reactivity between chemistries.

Rubella assay Primer/probe Sequence (5'-3') 1 RV98 GGCAGTTGGGTAAGAGACCA 2 RV251r CGTGGAGTGCTGGGTGAT 3 RV159rp /5Cy5/ACATCGCGCACTTCCCACG/3IAbRQSp/ World Health Organization, 2007. Manual for the laboratory diagnosis of measles and rubella virus infection. In Manual for the laboratory diagnosis of measles and rubella virus infection .

Hepatitis A assay Primer/probe Sequence (5'-3') 1 HAV-68-F TCACCGCCGTTTGCCTAG 2 HAV-240-R GGAGAGCCCTGGAAGAAAG 3 HAV-150-Probe /56-FAM/TTAATTCCT/ZEN/GCAGGTTCAGG/3IABkFQ/ Costafreda, M.I., Bosch, A. and Pintó, R.M., 2006. Development, evaluation, and standardization of a real-time TaqMan reverse transcription-PCR assay for quantification of hepatitis A virus in clinical and shellfish samples. Applied and environmental microbiology , 72 (6), pp.3846-3855.

Hepatitis E assay Primer/probe Sequence (5'-3') 1 JVHEVF GGTGGTTTCTGGGGTGAC 2 JVHEVR AGGGGTTGGTTGGATGA A 3 JVHEVPmod-2 /5TexRd-XN/TGATTCTCAGCCCTTCGC/3IAbRQSp/ Jothikumar, N., Cromeans, T.L., Robertson, B.H., Meng, X.J. and Hill, V.R., 2006. A broadly reactive one-step real-time RT-PCR assay for rapid and sensitive detection of hepatitis E virus. Journal of virological methods , 131 (1), pp.65-71.

Measles vaccine strain assay Primer/probe Sequence (5'-3') 1 VMA168F GAGATTGGGGGGCAAGGAAGAT 2 VMA242R GCATCACTTGCTCTGCTGGGCC 3 VMA190P FAM-AGGAGGGTC/BMN-Q535/AAACAGAGTCGA-MGB Ndlovu, N., Mabasa, V., Sankar, C., Msomi, N., Phalane, E., Singh, N., Gwala, S., Els, F., Macheke, M., Maposa, S. and Yousif, M., 2024. Wastewater testing during the South African 2022-2023 measles outbreak demonstrates the potential of environmental surveillance to support measles elimination. medRxiv , pp.2024-09.

Mpox generic assay Primer/probe Sequence (5'-3') 1 N3R-F TCCAATTATGACCTTTGATGGAA 2 N3R-R TGTTTAATTGTAGGACGGTTGT 3 N3R-P /56-FAM/TCCATCTAT/ZEN/CTCGTTCATGGTCGGT/3IABkFQ/ 4 B18R-F TGGTTGCCGGATCTACGTATT 5 B18R-R ACGTTCTTAGTCGGTGCATT 6 B18R-P /5Cy5/CTAGTTGCTAGATCTCTCGCTATCATAG/3BHQ-3/ Fan, Z., Xie, Y., Huang, B., Zhao, F., Hu, Y., Huang, Y., Mei, S., Wei, L., Wang, L., Wang, L. and Gao, Z., 2024. Development of a multiplex real-time PCR assay for the simultaneous detection of mpox virus and orthopoxvirus infections. Journal of Virological Methods , 328 , p.114957.
Assays ordered ready-made from Qiagen Assay name GeneGlobe ID Reporter 1 SARS-CoV-2 (N1) DMA00710 ROX 2 Vibrio Cholerae DMA00340 TAMRA 3 Dengue virus (pan) DMA00788 FAM 4 Hepatitis B DMA00808 FAM
Mycobacterium complex assay (IS6110) Primer/probe Sequence (5'-3') 1 TB_For TCCACGGCCTGGGCTACCA 2 TB_Rev CATCGCGCACGCATCGGG 3 TB_Probe /56-ROXN/CACTCCGCCGATCGTTTTTCC/3IABkFQ/

Mycobacterium tuberculosis assay (Glycosyltransferase) Primer/probe Sequence (5'-3') 1 Forward TCCACGGCCTGGGCTACCA 2 Reverse CATCGCGCACGCATCGGG 3 Probe /56-ROXN/CACTCCGCCGATCGTTTTTCC/3IABkFQ/ Park, J., Kwak, N., Chae, J.C., Yoon, E.J. and Jeong, S.H., 2023. A two-step real-time PCR method To identify Mycobacterium tuberculosis infections and six dominant nontuberculous mycobacterial infections from clinical specimens. Microbiology spectrum , 11 (4), pp.e01606-23.

Hepatitis B assay Primer/probe Sequence (5'-3') 1 HBV3-26F CGTGTTACAGGCGGKGTKTTTCTT 2 HBV60-83P /5Cy5/AGACTCGTG/TAO/GTGGACTTCTCTCAA/3IAbRQSp/ 3 HBV111-39R TGGAGRTTDGGGACTGCGAATTTWGGCCA Designed by Wastewater Genomics Syndicate at NICD, SA
